title: Brayan Bello named midseason AL ‘Cy Yuk’ winner by The Athletic for poor first-half performance
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Brayan Bello was named the AL midseason 'Cy Yuk' winner by The Athletic for his poor performance.
Bello posted a 10.35 ERA in eight starts for the Red Sox before being sent to Triple-A Worcester.
Despite starting struggles, Bello excelled in bullpen outings, achieving a 0.71 ERA in 25.1 innings.

title: Red Sox face all too familiar question heading into MLB trade deadline: Buy or sell?
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: The Boston Red Sox, on a seven-game winning streak, are two games short of a wild-card spot.
Despite earlier struggles, Boston's players, including Willson Contreras, have contributed to a strong mid-season rebound.
Red Sox management, led by Craig Breslow, must decide whether to buy or sell at the 2026 MLB trade deadline.

title: Review & Setlist: Shakira delivers high-energy nostalgia and new beginnings at TD Garden
author: Maddie Browning
bullets: Shakira performed at TD Garden on July 10, showcasing both nostalgic hits and tracks from her Grammy-winning album 'Las Mujeres Ya No Lloran'.
The concert included elaborate stage elements and outfits, featuring an inflatable she-wolf and several costume changes reminiscent of Selena and Cher.
Shakira's setlist spanned her career, highlighting songs like 'Hips Don't Lie', 'Whenever, Wherever', and 'Waka Waka', with a message of resilience and empowerment.

title: Healey announces tax credit awards to build almost 700 new homes in six Mass. cities
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Healey announces $18.6 million in tax credits for 662 new homes in six Massachusetts Gateway Cities.
Projects in Fall River, Lawrence, Lowell, Pittsfield, Taunton, and Worcester will convert historic buildings into residences.
The Housing Development Incentive Program aims to boost housing production and lower costs in designated Gateway Cities.

title: FBI arrests Mass. man in armed bank robberies in NH, NJ
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Joseph Sawyer, 52, was arrested by an FBI SWAT team in Albany, NY, for armed bank robberies.
Sawyer allegedly robbed St. Mary's Bank in Nashua, NH, and Chase Bank in Boonton, NJ, in June.
Police reported $6,000 stolen in Nashua and $12,000 in Boonton; FBI led a multi-state investigation.

title: Motorcyclist missing since July 4 found dead in NH
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Wesley Grondin, 41, was found dead near his motorcycle off Route 2 in Shelburne, NH.
Grondin had been missing since July 4, and his body was discovered by a resident on Thursday night.
Preliminary investigation suggests Grondin crossed into the eastbound lane and struck a post; no other vehicles involved.

title: Gov. Maura Healey declares July 11 ‘Noah Kahan Day’
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Gov. Maura Healey declares July 11 as 'Noah Kahan Day' in Massachusetts.
Kahan becomes first artist to headline four sold-out concerts at Fenway Park.
The proclamation honors Kahan's contributions to music and mental health advocacy.

title: ‘Let’s just call it a tour’: Judy Collins says her farewell tour isn’t a farewell
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: Judy Collins clarifies her 'farewell tour' isn't a farewell, calling it just another tour.
The 'Sweet Judy Blue Eyes' tour features stops in Cape Cod, Tanglewood, and will include all-star guests.
The tour will be filmed for a documentary on Collins's life, music, and legacy, extending through March 2027.

title: 3 new movies to watch on Netflix in July
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Netflix added 'A League of Their Own' (1992) featuring Geena Davis and Tom Hanks, set in 1940s women's pro baseball.
'Moneyball' (2011), starring Brad Pitt, follows Oakland Athletics GM Billy Beane's use of analytics in baseball.
'Gone Girl' (2014) includes Ben Affleck and Rosamund Pike in a mystery about a missing wife and media scrutiny.

title: Red Sox win 7th straight game just hours after landing in New York
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: The Boston Red Sox defeated the New York Mets 6-2, marking their seventh consecutive win and improving their record to 44-48.
Sonny Gray pitched six innings, allowing one run, contributing to his 11th win of the season for the Red Sox.
Despite travel delays, Anthony Seigler and Wilyer Abreu hit key home runs in the Red Sox's win over the Mets.
